speaker: DOCTOR
text: Hello, Mr. Smith. What's going on today?

speaker: PATIENT
text: I've been short of breath. It came on pretty suddenly.

speaker: PATIENT
text: This morning, maybe around eight. I was just sitting at my desk.

speaker: PATIENT
text: A little. It's sharp, and it's worse when I take a deep breath.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Any cough, fever, or leg swelling?

speaker: PATIENT
text: No cough, no fever. But my right calf has been sore for a few days.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Have you traveled recently or been immobile for long periods?

speaker: PATIENT
text: I flew back from Europe last week. It was a long flight, maybe nine hours.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: You said it came on this morning — was it really sudden, or did it build up?

speaker: PATIENT
text: Actually, now that I think about it, I noticed it a bit yesterday evening too. It just got much worse this morning.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Got it. Any history of asthma, heart problems, or clots?

speaker: PATIENT
text: No asthma. My father had a DVT, I think.

speaker: PATIENT
text: I take lisinopril for blood pressure, and I'm on a daily aspirin.

speaker: PATIENT
text: I quit about ten years ago. Smoked for twenty years before that.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: I want to check your oxygen saturation and listen to your lungs.
